source: All-In Podcast — 2025 predictions (bestie guest Gavin Baker)
summary: M&A wave is coming after four years of regulatory suppression; Intel will be subject to a significant deal
summary_extended: Baker predicts a 'tidal wave' of M&A activity in 2025 driven by four years of pent-up deal demand suppressed by aggressive antitrust enforcement under the prior regulatory regime. He specifically singles out Intel as a company where 'something significant will happen,' framing it as his biggest business deal prediction of the year and expressing hope the outcome benefits America — implying a strategic acquisition or merger rather than a breakup or failure. Baker also predicts that independent frontier AI labs will be acquired, suggesting consolidation across both semiconductors and AI. The investment implication is that INTC is a likely M&A target, making it a potential long on deal optionality, though Baker does not name specific acquirers. No specific price targets, deal structures, or acquirer names are cited, leaving the thesis directional rather than precise.
detail: Baker predicts a 'tidal wave' of M&A due to pent-up demand after four years of blocked deals. He specifically calls out Intel as a company where 'something will happen' and expresses hope it will be good for America. He also predicts independent frontier AI labs will be acquired.
confidence_evidence: Baker names Intel specifically and frames M&A broadly as his biggest business deal prediction
quote_fragment: something will happen with Intel and that will be big and hopefully it's good for America

ticker_audit.reasoning: Baker explicitly names Intel as the specific company where 'something will happen' and calls it his biggest business deal prediction, making INTC the direct subject of this M&A thesis.
